description: Recto: Legal document. In Hebrew. Location: Cairo. Dated: First decade of Kislev 5439 AM = November 1678 CE. It has to do with property and financial arrangements following the death of Levi Griyānī (גריאני) known as Ḥefeẓ. His orphans are David, Nissim, and Yiṣḥaq. Levi's widow Esther (also now deceased) used to reside in one of the properties, a shop in Sūq al-Kharuzātiyya of Cairo (spelled here as: סוק אלכ'ריזאתיין). This market near the center of early modern Cairo was a hub for goldsmith shops, in particular (Raymond, Artisans et Comerçants, 337). Another property from the deceased man's estate is on the street of the Maghrebi congregation (דרב ק"ק מערביים). Verso: Legal document. In Hebrew. Location: Cairo. Dated: First third of Nisan 5439 AM, which is 1679 CE. Involves the wives of David and Yiṣḥaq Griyānī, who are respectively Qamr bt. Avraham Dukkān and Simḥa bt. Shemuel Simḥon. The properties from "the other side" (מעבר הלז) of the document are referenced in connection with the Qamr and Simḥa's ketubbōt. For paleographical confirmation of Benyamīn Shaḥata's intricate signature, see BL OR 5561A.4–4a (PGPID 19226) where he refers to himself in the first person as scribe (ll. 6, 9). ASE/MCD.
description_he: פנים: מסמך משפטי בעברית. מקום: קהיר. תאריך: עשרת הימים הראשונים של כסלו ה'תל"ט = נובמבר 1678 לספירה. הסדרים רכושיים כספיים לאחר פטירת לוי גריאני הידוע כחפץ. יתומיו: דוד, נסים ויצחק. רכוש בשוק אל-ח'ריזאתיין ובדרב ק"ק מערביים. גב: מסמך משפטי בעברית מאדר ה'תל"ט = 1679, הנוגע לנשות דוד ויצחק גריאני ולכתובותיהן.
